checks.a_analysis: The HTML conversion follows the slide-by-slide sequence of the original PDF. The transition from a landscape presentation format to a vertical single-column HTML layout is an expected responsive design choice and does not negatively impact the logical organization of information. Information flow is consistent with the source document.

The tables in the document, particularly the 'Improvement from Stop Consolidation' table on page 16 and the 'Here's your tally' grid on page 17, are rendered correctly in the HTML. The conversion also successfully transformed complex software form screenshots (Page 9) and report text (Page 11) into readable HTML tables. The data remains understandable and visually structured.

Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML conversion. Specifically, the majority of GIS-related maps and one key photo are absent. This includes the map images on slides 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 22, and 23, which are central to the 'The Value of GIS' theme. Additionally, the photo of a school bus at a railroad crossing on slide 11 is missing.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains several critical images including: a photo of a bus at railroad tracks (page 11), a map of NCDOT railroad crossings (page 12), a bus run optimization map (page 13), bus stop consolidation maps (page 14), bus route maps (page 15), a school boundary planning map (page 17), a median income map (page 22), and a student growth heat map (page 23).
checks.b_evidence_output: The corresponding sections in the HTML output (e.g., 'Bus Routing Information', 'NCDOT Railroad Crossing Report', 'Bus Run Optimization', 'Case Study', 'School Boundary Planning', etc.) are missing these primary visual elements, showing only the text or associated tables.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically pages 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 22, and 23.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of unintentional content duplication in the HTML output. While the title slide content appears at the beginning and end, this reflects the structure of the source PDF itself (slides 1 and 24). Within individual slides, the body content is not repeated.
